title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Go Client Systems · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An on-call reliability engineer tracing a production client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go 1.26 net/http representation negotiation and validation boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regr…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Order the regression proof · Debug Language Charset Negotiation
steps.instruction:
Order the Language Charset Negotiation Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Order the regression proof · Debug Language Charset Negotiation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Language Charset Negotiation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Order the regression proof · Debug Language Charset Negotiation, keep Language Charset Negotiation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go incident collection agent asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:2. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

hints: For Go Publish the incident record · Debug Language Charset Negotiation, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Language Charset Negotiation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go Publish the incident record · Debug Language Charset Negotiation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: Go incident collection agent asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:5. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Publish the incident record · Debug Language Charset Negotiation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: JSON, text, bytes, Unicode code points, and user-perceived characters are interchangeable. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
